DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CARLOS DEL OLMO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTGERROGATORIES PROPOUNDED TO PLAINTIFF LESLIE LAPAYOWKE. THE COURT FINDS THAT PRIOR NAMES, BIRTH DATE AND PLACE, PRIOR RESIDENCE ADDRESSES, AND EDUCATIONAL BACKGROUND COULD NOT BE USED TO LOCATE AND HARASS THE PLAINTIFF. THEREFORE, UNLESS CURRENT ADDRESS AND EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS ARE REQUIRED TO BE DISCLOSED, IT IS UNNECESSARY TO RULE ON THE APPLICABILITY OR EFFECT OF CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTION ARTICLE I, SECTION 28(B)(4). THE COURT FURTHER FINDS INFORMATION CONCERNING CURRENT ADDRESS AND EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS IS NOT ESSENTIAL FOR DEFENDANT'S PURPOSES. ACCORDINGLY THE COURT WILL GRANT THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES 2.1, 2.2, 2.5, AND 2.6, WITHOUT DISCLOSURE OF CURRENT ADDRESSES OR EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THE MEDICAL AND BILLING RECORDS PRODUCED ARE INSUFFICIENTLY COMPLETE, LEGIBLE, ORGANIZED, OR IDENTIFIED TO SERVE IN LIEU OF RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES 6.5 AND 6.7. ACCORDINGLY THE COURT WILL ALSO GRANT THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THOSE INTERROGATORIES. IN ORDER TO AFFORD PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO SEEK A CIVIL HARASSMENT RESTRAINING ORDER OR OTHER PROTECTION, SAID FURTHER RESPONSES WILL BE DUE 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF ORDER HEREON, UNLESS FURTHER EXTENDED BY STIPULATION OR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES FLEMING, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF LESLIE LAPAYOWKER'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER THE COURT'S RULINGS ON APRIL 8, 2019 AND ALTERNATIVELY MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP SEC 473. PLAINTIFF LESLIE LAPAYOWKER'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER THE COURT'S APRIL 8, 2019 ORDERS AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CODE CIV. PROC. 473 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT ANY "NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW" WARRANT RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S RULINGS. (CODE CIV. PROC. 1008(A).)ON MARCH 11, 2019, DEFENDANT TIMELY FILED AND SERVED ITS MOTIONS FOR AN ORDER ADOPTING THE TENTATIVE RULING OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR AN ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER. Plaintiff'S Motion To Reconsider The Court'S Rulings On April 8, 2019 And Alternatively Motion For Relief Under Ccp Sec 473
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LESLIE LAPAYOWKER's Motion To Reconsider The Court'S Rulings On April 8, 2019 And Alternatively Motion For Relief Under Ccp Sec 473.

Plaintiff Leslie Lapayowker's motion to reconsider the Court's April 8, 2019 orders and alternative motion for relief under Code Civ. Proc. 473 is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that any "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" warrant reconsideration of the Court's rulings. (Code Civ. Proc. 1008(a).) On March 11, 2019, Defendant timely filed and served its motions for an order adopting the tentative ruling of the judge pro tem on Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and for an order denying Plaintiff's motion for protective order. Both motions were set for hearing on April 8, 2019. Plaintiff did not file any opposition to either motion. Even if that was an excusable oversight that in light of the prior default and any confusion relating to the posting of the judge pro tem's tentative ruling, Plaintiff does not offer any reason for failing to contest the Court's tentative rulings posted on April 7, 2019, or for failing to appear at the hearing on April 8. Plaintiff therefore has failed to show any basis for relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 473.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEFENDANT CARLOS DEL OLMO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Intgerrogatories Propounded To Plaintiff Leslie Lapayowke.

The Court finds that prior names, birth date and place, prior residence addresses, and educational background could not be used to locate and harass the plaintiff. Accordingly the Court will grant the motion to compel further responses to Interrogatories 2.1, 2.2, 2.5, and 2.6, without disclosure of current addresses or educational institutions.

Defendant Carlos Del Olmos'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles County; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Carlos Del Olmo; Declaration Of Angel Alvarez; Declaration Of Christina Vanarelli (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CARLOS DEL OLMO'S Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles County is DENIED. Venue is determined at the action's outset from the original complaint, even though, as here, a defendant that confers venue is later dismissed. (See Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 3:489.) As to convenience, only the convenience of nonparty witnesses is important, and the nonparty witnesses here reside in Florida and Texas; i.e., a San Francisco venue would essentially be as convenient as Los Angeles. (See id. at 3:555.)

ORDER SIGNED in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, email: mtorreano@sftc.org; torreanom@aol.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Daniel Pires; Declaration Of Carlos Del Olmo; Declaration Of Christina Vanarelli
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CARLOS DEL OLMO'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default against him on February 13, 2018 is GRANTED. Mr. Del Olmo provides sufficient evidence to meet the extremely low burden to set aside a default on a promptly filed motion per CCP 473(b). (Shamblin v. Brattan (1988) 44 Cal. 3d 474, 478 ("when a party in default moves promptly to seek relief, very slight evidence is required to justify a trial court's order setting aside a default.")). Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and there is no discernable legal prejudice to plaintiff Leslie Lapayowker by the granting of the motion.

ORDER SIGNED.
